title: Artemis 2 swings around the moon
summary: Four astronauts from the U.S. and Canada completed a lunar flyby as part of the Artemis 2 mission, marking the farthest human travel from Earth since Apollo. This mission is a key step toward NASA's goal of returning humans to the moon.

title: Moog Highlights Growing Satellite Bus Capabilities with Full‑Scale METEOR Reveal at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Inc. showcased its full-scale METEOR satellite bus at the Space Symposium, highlighting advancements in satellite bus capabilities. The reveal underscores Moog's growing role in the satellite industry.

title: FOSSA targets Japan’s defense market as larger smallsats expand capabilities
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems is entering Japan's defense market with the help of a local partner, expanding beyond its initial focus on tiny picosatellites to more capable spacecraft for government use.

title: The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.
summary: A moon base project has a hardware plan but lacks a software strategy. The article highlights the importance of software development alongside hardware in space missions.

title: Seagate Space Signs MOU with Firefly Aerospace to Collaborate on Offshore Launch Infrastructure for Alpha
summary: Seagate Space Corporation and Firefly Aerospace have signed an MOU to develop offshore launch infrastructure for Firefly's Alpha rocket. The collaboration aims to enhance launch capabilities from maritime platforms.

title: Military space programs confront hidden supply constraints
summary: Military space programs face potential supply chain bottlenecks due to hidden constraints in critical components like optical links and valves. An industry report highlights these chokepoints as growing concerns for program execution.

title: Artemis 2 ready to fly around the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 mission is scheduled to fly around the moon on April 6, aiming to set a distance record while astronauts study the lunar farside.

title: Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman defended a proposed 25% budget cut for fiscal year 2027, despite significant reductions to the agency's funding.

title: NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2
summary: NASA has halted construction on the second Mobile Launcher (ML-2) for the Space Launch System (SLS) due to the cancellation of an upgraded version of the rocket. The decision reflects a shift in NASA's Artemis program priorities.

title: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
summary: NASA is finalizing science plans for observations to be conducted by astronauts during the Artemis 2 lunar flyby mission. The mission marks a key step toward future lunar exploration.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts fly around the moon in record-breaking lunar loop by NASA
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts completed a record-breaking lunar flyby, viewing parts of the moon never before seen by humans. The mission set a new spaceflight milestone during their journey around the moon.

title: Moon music: Here's a Spotify playlist of the Artemis 2 crew's wakeup songs
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 crew has a Spotify playlist of wake-up songs to start their day with positivity during the mission. The playlist is shared by mission control as part of the crew's daily routine.

title: Moon memorial: Artemis 2 astronauts name lunar 'bright spot' after mission commander's late wife
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 crew named a lunar 'bright spot' after the mission commander's late wife during their record-breaking journey. The dedication was part of a moment of remembrance while traveling the farthest distance from Earth ever by astronauts.

title: Artemis 2 breaks humanity's all-time distance record during historic loop around the moon (video)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has broken the record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth by humans, surpassing the Apollo 13 crew's 1970 achievement. The historic milestone was achieved during a loop around the moon.

title: 'Star Wars: Maul – Shadow Lord' is Lucasfilm's darkest and most focused animated show yet
summary: Lucasfilm's animated series 'Star Wars: Maul – Shadow Lord' is praised as the darkest and most focused show yet, but faces typical debut season issues. The series centers on Darth Maul and distinguishes itself from other Star Wars properties.

title: The moon up close: How the Artemis 2 astronauts are photographing their historic lunar flyby
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts will be the first humans to photograph and survey certain areas of the moon's far side during their historic lunar flyby. They are documenting their mission in detail.

title: Deja vu: Trump proposes cutting NASA science funding by 47% again
summary: NASA faces significant budget cuts in the FY 2027 request, with a 23% overall reduction and a 47% cut to science programs. This marks a recurring proposal to slash NASA's science funding.

title: Astronaut Victor Glover is the latest in a long line of Black American explorers − including York, the enslaved man who played a key role in the Lewis and Clark expedition
summary: Astronaut Victor Glover is celebrated as part of a long history of Black American explorers, including York from the Lewis and Clark expedition. His achievement highlights an underappreciated legacy in space exploration.

title: NASA Artemis 2 astronauts to make historic moon flyby today. Here's what to expect hour by hour (timeline)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are set to make a historic moon flyby today, with a detailed hour-by-hour timeline of the event. This marks a significant milestone in the Artemis program's mission to return humans to the moon.

title: Humanity is back at the moon! Artemis 2 astronauts arrive in lunar space
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have reached the moon's sphere of influence, marking humanity's return to lunar space. The four crew members entered this region early Monday morning (April 6).

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew has set a new record for the farthest human spaceflight, surpassing previous distances. The Orion spacecraft's final lunar flyby was adjusted to approximately 4,067 miles from the lunar surface.

title: NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview
summary: NASA's Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission will deliver around 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. This is the second flight of the upgraded Cygnus XL spacecraft, which offers increased cargo capacity.

title: Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II
summary: The Night Sky Network is celebrating Artemis II as the crew reaches a milestone, traveling farther than any humans in recorded history by orbiting the far side of the Moon. The event will be broadcast live on NASA’s YouTube channel and NASA+ on April 6, 2026.

title: Grants
summary: NASA has provided instructions for submitting grant status requests through a General Information Request Form, which includes automated email responses for common inquiries. The agency also issued guidance for grantees regarding OMB Memorandum M-25-14 and recent temporary restraining orders.

title: The Near Side of the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft captured a photo of the Moon's near side on April 4, 2026, showcasing ancient lava flows. The Artemis II astronauts Victor Glover, Reid Wiseman, and Christina Koch are mentioned in the context of this mission.

title: Thinking of You, Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, scheduled for April 4, 2026, will see astronaut Reid Wiseman, along with Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and CSA astronaut, traveling towards the Moon and observing Earth from the Orion spacecraft's window.

title: NASA Answers Your Most Pressing Artemis II Questions
summary: NASA updated details for the Artemis II mission, including corrected timings for lunar flyby activities and a distance record. The changes were announced in two updates on April 5 and 6, 2026.

title: Illuminated in Orion
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen are seen inside the Orion crew capsule in a photo taken in April 2026. The image highlights the dimly lit interior of the spacecraft, with lights turned off to avoid glare.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply launch to the International Space Station. The mission will carry 11,000 pounds of cargo aboard a Northrop Grumman Cygnus spacecraft launched by a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its midpoint.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft races toward a historic lunar flyby. This marks the first crewed mission in the Artemis program, carrying astronauts on a journey around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is highlighted for its role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration underscores the company's contribution to deep space exploration efforts.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California as part of the Transporter 16 mission. This marks a significant deployment of small satellites for various customers.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway program. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned as a source for space industry news. No specific details about the news content are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. This innovation could enhance satellite and spacecraft capabilities.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25 decisions with industry stakeholders. These events will outline planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communications.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete.

title: Artemis II Supplier Series: Orion’s Windows
summary: The article discusses the significance of the windows on the Orion spacecraft for the Artemis II mission, highlighting their importance for astronauts' experience in space. It is part of a series focusing on suppliers for the Artemis II mission.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the 2026 request. The budget includes reductions to NASA's science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts deeming it a 'show stopper' during an industry seminar. The lack of adequate insurance options poses significant risks for these ventures.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and statistics defining the space industry in Q1 2026, highlighting a resurgence in Earth Observation (EO) activities. It emphasizes the growing importance of EO in the commercial space sector.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a vision for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year, which has energized CLPS companies. These companies are eager to participate in the initiative.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications. This acquisition aims to enhance Phantom Space's capabilities in satellite technology.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with a crew including the first woman and person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The mission marks a historic step in space exploration.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding to develop an uncrewed space station for hosting manufacturing infrastructure.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other investors.

title: Artemis II Crew Travels Farther from Earth Than Apollo 13 in a New Record
summary: The Artemis II crew has traveled farther from Earth than any previous human mission, surpassing the Apollo 13 distance record. This milestone marks a significant achievement for NASA's Artemis program.

title: Trump 2027 Budget Request Targets Cuts for NASA, Boost for Golden Dome
summary: The White House's fiscal 2027 budget proposal includes significant cuts to NASA while proposing a major increase in defense spending, particularly for the Golden Dome missile defense initiative.

title: How Iceye Satellites Track Russian Vessels, Illegal Shipping in the Arctic
summary: Iceye satellites are being used to monitor Russian vessels and illegal shipping activities in the Arctic. The technology enables tracking of submarines and ships even in challenging conditions like dark nights and cloud cover.

title: L3Harris and Comtech Release Multi-Orbit Tactical Satcom Modem
summary: L3Harris and Comtech have released a new multi-orbit tactical satcom embeddable modem that works with certain L3Harris terminals. The modem was announced on March 31.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium CEO Matt Desch announced the rollout of Iridium NTN Direct, positioning Iridium as the first global, standardized narrow-band satellite IoT service. The initiative also highlights advances in alternative PNT (Positioning, Navigation, and Timing) technologies.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has won a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for the European Space Agency’s Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program. This initiative aims to enhance secure communications in Europe.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass of 11.7 million square miles has historically posed challenges for satellite services, but the market is now experiencing pent-up demand as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to acquire Globalstar, a satellite communications company. The news was first reported by the Financial Times.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. The funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force’s innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate on a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing to enhance satellite removal capabilities.
